# Habitify: Habit Tracker · Habit Tracker

Generated: Jul 22, 2026, 11:08 AM

## **Contents** {#toc}

- [Bugs and stability](#theme-bugs_stability)
- [Pricing and paywall](#theme-pricing_paywall)


## **Bugs and stability** {#theme-bugs_stability}

   **Bugs and stability.**
      4 recent review(s) mention bugs and stability.
      Review window: Mar 23, 2026 - Jun 10, 2026
      **Why it matters:** Users report reliability problems. This points to quality gaps competitors can exploit.

         **Review examples:**
            1. _ADWired_, Jun 10, 2026, 2/5: "I tried this app for a day during the trial period and liked it enough to purchase a one-year subscription. Unfortunately, shortly after subscribing, the app became unstable. It frequently logs me out, the widgets have never worked (they appear blank), and Apple Health syncing stopped working after the first day. I contacted support and initially received a quick response with some follow-up questions, but after providing the requested information, I never received a solution or any further assistance. I tried reinstalling the app multiple times, but the issues persisted. Out of frustration, I canceled my subscription and requested a refund. My refund request was denied. As a result, I paid for a subscription to an app that does not function properly, received no resolution for the problems I reported, and was then denied a refund. This has been a very disappointing customer experience."
            2. _HectorZeroni03_, Apr 29, 2026, 2/5: "App crashes spontaneously 20APR2025 edit: Communication to the support staff is good as they respond about once a day. That being said resolution of bugs issues is slow (i guess to be expected, but unfortunate due to the 28$ annual fee). The app has so much potential which is why it’s so frustrating with its somewhat blatant lack of attention to detail."

## **Pricing and paywall** {#theme-pricing_paywall}

   **Pricing and paywall.**
      2 recent review(s) mention pricing and paywall.
      Review window: Feb 18, 2026 - Jun 10, 2026
      **Why it matters:** Users mention price, subscriptions, or paywalls. This can signal pricing sensitivity in the niche.

         **Review examples:**
            1. _ADWired_, Jun 10, 2026, 2/5: "I tried this app for a day during the trial period and liked it enough to purchase a one-year subscription. Unfortunately, shortly after subscribing, the app became unstable. It frequently logs me out, the widgets have never worked (they appear blank), and Apple Health syncing stopped working after the first day. I contacted support and initially received a quick response with some follow-up questions, but after providing the requested information, I never received a solution or any further assistance. I tried reinstalling the app multiple times, but the issues persisted. Out of frustration, I canceled my subscription and requested a refund. My refund request was denied. As a result, I paid for a subscription to an app that does not function properly, received no resolution for the problems I reported, and was then denied a refund. This has been a very disappointing customer experience."
            2. _…24680_, Feb 18, 2026, 5/5: "I’m not certain that any habit tracker is perfect for most people, still less all. But having tried probably twenty to thirty over the years, this is the one that I’ve at settled with. It’s the only one I’ve found that has a decent UX, can allow collaboration and isn’t *tremendously* expensive (the limitations on the free version of course make that fairly pointless, but the subscription or lifetime options aren’t bad compared with some similar apps’ pricing.) I had a minor issue with some of the settings (due to my misunderstanding) and support were lightning fast with helpful info, so that was a plus."
